name: Abel Beth Maacah
description: Northern covenant city; Sheba’s head thrown over the wall.
nameMeaning: Brook of the house of Maacah.

scripture.text: Sheba passed through all the tribes of Israel toward Abel Beth Maakah, through the entire region of the Bikrites, who gathered together and followed him.

name: Abel Shittim
description: Moabite plains camp east of Jordan; Israel’s final stop before crossing.

scripture.text: They camped on the Moabite plateau, along the Jordan across from Jericho between Beth Jeshimoth and Abel Shittim.

name: Adramyttium
description: Mysian port; ship of Adramyttium in custody voyage.

scripture.text: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia, and we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, was with us.

name: Adullam
description: Shephelah town; David’s cave stronghold while fleeing Saul.
nameMeaning: Possibly ‘justice of El’ or witness of El.

scripture.text: David left Gath and escaped to the cave of Adullam. When his brothers and his father’s household heard about it, they went down to him there.

name: Ai (near Bethel)
description: Canaanite town taken in Joshua’s southern sweep after Jericho.

scripture.text: Now Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai, which is near Beth Aven to the east of Bethel.

name: Ammon
description: Lot’s descendants through Ben-Ammi; frequent conflict and marriage tensions with Israel.

scripture.text: Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty warrior. He was the son of a prostitute, and his father Gilead.

name: Amphipolis
description: Strategic bridge city on the Strymon; Paul passes through toward Thessalonica.

scripture.text: When Paul and his companions had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.

name: Anathoth
description: Levitical town in Benjamin; Jeremiah’s hometown.

scripture.text: The words of Jeremiah son of Hilkiah, one of the priests at Anathoth in the territory of Benjamin.

name: Antioch on the Orontes
description: First great Gentile church; sending base for Paul’s missions.

scripture.text: The disciples were called Christians first at Antioch.

name: Antipatris
description: Herodian plain fortress; Paul escorted night march toward Caesarea.

scripture.text: So the soldiers, carrying out their orders, took Paul with them during the night and brought him as far as Antipatris.

name: Apamea (Phrygia)
description: Great inland market city on the royal road; Roman Asia assembly point.

scripture.text: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the region of Phrygia and Galatia, having been kept by the Holy Spirit from preaching the word in the province of Asia.

name: Apollonia (Macedonia)
description: Coastal waystation between Amphipolis and Thessalonica.

scripture.text: When Paul and his companions had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.

name: Aphek (1 Samuel 4)
description: Philistine camp opposite Israel in the battle where the ark was captured.

scripture.text: Now the Israelites went out to fight against the Philistines. The Israelites camped at Ebenezer, and the Philistines at Aphek.

name: Beth Shemesh
description: Border town where the ark returned on the cart from Philistia; some men struck down for irreverence.

scripture.text: Then the cows went straight up toward Beth Shemesh, and they followed along the highway, lowing as they went.

name: Ebenezer (1 Samuel 4)
description: Israelite camp where the ark was brought against the Philistines; Israel defeated and ark taken.
events: Ark brought from Shiloh to battle

scripture.text: And when the people came to the camp, the elders of Israel said, ‘Why did the Lord bring defeat on us today before the Philistines? Let us bring the ark of the covenant of the Lord here from Shiloh, so that he may go with us and save us from the hand of our enemies.’

name: Ashdod
description: Philistine stronghold; Philistia’s plain power center.

scripture.text: Moreover, in those days I saw men of Judah who had married women from Ashdod, Ammon and Moab.

name: Ashkelon (coast)
description: Philistine pentapolis on the sea; Samson’s riddle aftermath.

scripture.text: Tell it not in Gath, proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Philistines be glad, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.

name: Assos
description: Troad harbor; Paul walked overland while Luke sailed.

scripture.text: We went on ahead to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we were going to take Paul aboard. He had made this arrangement because he was going there on foot.

name: Athens
description: Classical intellectual center; Paul’s Areopagus speech.

scripture.text: Paul then stood up in the meeting of the Areopagus and said: ‘People of Athens! I see that in every way you are very religious.’

name: Azekah
description: Fortified Judahite city in the Shephelah; near David and Goliath’s valley.

scripture.text: The Lord threw them into confusion before Israel, so great a defeat at Gibeon that they fled from before Israel, toward the road to Beth Horon and Azekah and Makkedah.

name: Babylon
description: Capital of Neo-Babylonian empire; Judah’s exile.

scripture.text: Nebuchadnezzar carried Jehoiachin captive to Babylon.

name: Beer Lahai Roi
description: Well where the angel met Hagar.
nameMeaning: Well of the Living One who sees me.

scripture.text: That is why the well was called Beer Lahai Roi; it is still there, between Kadesh and Bered.

name: Beersheba
description: Southern gateway of the promised land; associated with the patriarchs.

scripture.text: So that place was called Beersheba, because the two men swore an oath there.

name: Beersheba wells
description: Oath wells between Abraham and Abimelek; southern anchor of ‘Dan to Beersheba.’
nameMeaning: Well of the seven / well of the oath.

scripture.text: So that place was called Beersheba, because the two men swore an oath there.

name: Berea
description: Macedonian synagogue city; nobler reception of Scripture than Thessalonica.

scripture.text: Now the Berean Jews were of more noble character than those in Thessalonica, for they received the message with great eagerness and examined the Scriptures every day to see if what Paul said was true.

name: Beth Shean
description: Strategic Jezreel junction; Philistines displayed Saul’s body.

scripture.text: They put his armor in the temple of the Ashtoreths and fastened his body to the wall of Beth Shan.

name: Beth Zur
description: Fortified ascent town guarding the Hebron–Jerusalem road; Maccabean wars.

scripture.text: Halhul, Beth Zur, Gederah,

name: Bethany
description: Mary, Martha, and Lazarus; Jesus’ ascent toward Jerusalem.
nameMeaning: Hebrew Beth Aniah — “house of the poor / affliction” (traditional).

scripture.text: Now a man named Lazarus was sick. He lived in Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.

name: Bethel
description: Jacob’s ‘house of God’ vision; later Jeroboam’s golden calves nearby.

scripture.text: He called that place Bethel, though the city used to be called Luz.

name: Bethel / Beth Aven
description: Border town name used prophetically for ‘house of trouble’ near Bethel.
nameMeaning: House of vanity / trouble ( Hosea wordplay on Bethel).

scripture.text: Though you, Israel, commit adultery, do not let Judah become guilty. Do not go to Gilgal; do not go to Beth Aven. And do not swear, ‘As surely as the Lord lives!’

name: Bethlehem
description: Judah’s royal village; birthplace of David and of Jesus the Messiah.

scripture.text: But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, though you are small among the clans of Judah, out of you will come for me one who will be ruler over Israel.
After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, Magi from the east came to Jerusalem.

name: Bethphage
modernName: Mount of Olives east slope, Jerusalem
description: Starting point for Jesus’ Palm Sunday approach.

scripture.text: As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,

name: Bethsaida
description: Fishing village; home of Philip, Andrew, Peter (tradition).

scripture.text: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

name: Brundisium
description: Heel-of-Italy port paired with Dyrrachium on the Ignatian/Appian corridor.

scripture.text: When it was decided that we would sail for Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were handed over to a centurion named Julius, who belonged to the Imperial Regiment.

name: Caesarea Maritima
description: Herodian port city; Roman administration; center of early Gentile mission.

scripture.text: At Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion in what was known as the Italian Regiment.

name: Caesarea Philippi (Banias)
description: Pan sanctuary region; Peter’s confession at ‘this rock.’
events: Who do you say I am?

scripture.text: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, ‘Who do people say the Son of Man is?’

name: Cana of Galilee
description: Wedding at Cana; Nathanael’s hometown tradition.

scripture.text: On the third day a wedding took place at Cana in Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there,

name: Capernaum
description: Jesus’ ministry base on the Sea of Galilee.

scripture.text: Leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which was by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.

name: Cenchreae
description: Corinth’s eastern harbor; Phoebe’s church and Paul’s hair vow.
nameMeaning: From Greek Cenchreae (millet / plant name tradition).

scripture.text: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a deacon of the church in Cenchreae.

name: Chios
description: Aegean island; Paul’s return toward Miletus.

scripture.text: Sailing from there, we arrived the next day opposite Chios. The day after that we crossed over to Samos, and on the following day arrived at Miletus.

name: Cnidus
description: Carian peninsula city; storm voyage rounds here.

scripture.text: We moved about slowly for many days and had difficulty arriving off Cnidus. When the wind did not allow us to hold our course, we sailed to the lee of Crete opposite Salmone.

name: Colossae
description: Lycus valley city; letter to Colossians.

scripture.text: To God’s holy people in Colossae, the faithful brothers and sisters in Christ: Grace and peace to you from God our Father.

name: Corinth
description: Wealthy Roman colony; Paul’s longest correspondence.

scripture.text: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.

name: Cos (Kos)
description: Asclepius cult island; Paul’s voyage toward Rhodes.

scripture.text: After we had torn ourselves away from them, we put out to sea and sailed straight to Cos. The next day we went to Rhodes and from there to Patara.

name: Cush (Upper Nile / Ethiopia)
description: Biblical Cush south of Egypt; eunuch from Candace’s court.
nameMeaning: Ethiopian region name (Cush son of Ham).

scripture.text: Woe to the land of whirring wings along the rivers of Cush,

name: Damascus
description: Major Aramean city; Saul’s conversion on the road from here.

scripture.text: As he neared Damascus on his journey, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.

name: Dan (Laish)
description: Northernmost Israelite shrine; tribe migrated north and renamed Laish.

scripture.text: They named it Dan after their ancestor Dan, who was born to Israel—though the city used to be called Laish.

name: Dead Sea
description: Salt sea at the Great Rift; cities of the plain were nearby.

scripture.text: All these latter kings joined forces in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Dead Sea).

name: Debir (Kiriath Sepher)
modernName: Tell Beit Mirsim area, West Bank
description: Hill-country city taken in Joshua’s southern campaign; Othniel’s prize.

scripture.text: From there, Judah attacked the people living in Debir (formerly called Kiriath Sepher).

name: Derbe
description: Eastern Lycaonian city visited on Paul’s journeys.

scripture.text: But after the disciples had gathered around him, he got up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas left for Derbe.

name: Dothan
description: Joseph sold by brothers; Elisha’s surrounded servant.

scripture.text: So Joseph went after his brothers and found them near Dothan. But they saw him in the distance, and before he reached them, they plotted to kill him.

name: Dyrrachium (Epirus)
description: Adriatic port opposite Brundisium; Illyricum mission horizon.

scripture.text: So from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum, I have fully proclaimed the gospel of Christ.

name: Edom (Seir)
description: Esau’s descendants; rocky kingdom south and east of the Dead Sea.

scripture.text: The vision of Obadiah. This is what the Sovereign Lord says about Edom—We have heard a message from the Lord: An envoy was sent to the nations to say, ‘Rise, let us go against her for battle’

name: Ekron
description: Philistine border city; ark returned nearby.
events: Ark to Beth Shemesh via Ekron

scripture.text: So they sent the ark of God to Ekron. As the ark of God was entering Ekron, the people of Ekron cried out, ‘They have brought the ark of the god of Israel around to us to kill us and our people.’

name: Emmaus (Nicopolis tradition)
description: Road where risen Jesus walked with disciples (site debated).

scripture.text: Now that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.

name: En Gannim
modernName: Ein Ganim near Jenin (schematic), West Bank
description: Border town in Issachar or allotment list; ‘spring of gardens.’

scripture.text: Remeth, En Gannim, En Haddah and Beth Pazzez.

name: En Gedi
description: Dead Sea spring oasis where David hid from Saul.

scripture.text: After Saul returned from pursuing the Philistines, he was told, ‘David is in the Desert of En Gedi.’

name: En Rogel
description: Spring at the Kidron–Hinnom junction; spies and Adonijah’s rebellion.

scripture.text: Both Jonathan and Ahimaaz stayed at En Rogel. A female servant was to go and inform them, and they were to go and tell King David, for they could not risk being seen entering the city.

name: Endor
description: Jezreel foothill settlement; medium of Endor.

scripture.text: Saul then said to his attendants, ‘Find me a woman who is a medium, so I may go and inquire of her.’ ‘There is one in Endor,’ they said.

name: Ephesus
description: Major Artemis cult city; Paul’s Asian base.

scripture.text: While Apollos was at Corinth, Paul took the road through the interior and arrived at Ephesus.

name: Eshtaol
description: Danite border town; Samson’s spirit stirred between here and Zorah.

scripture.text: and the Spirit of the Lord began to stir him while he was in Mahaneh Dan, between Zorah and Eshtaol.

name: Fair Havens (Crete)
description: South Cretan roadstead where the centurion’s ship waited before the storm.

scripture.text: We moved along the coast with difficulty and came to a place called Fair Havens, near the town of Lasea.

name: Forum of Appius
description: Market town on the Appian Way south of Rome; Christians met Paul.

scripture.text: The brothers and sisters there had heard that we were coming, and they traveled as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us.

name: Gath
description: Philistine pentapolis; Goliath’s hometown; David fled here.

scripture.text: A champion named Goliath, who was from Gath, came out of the Philistine camp. His height was six cubits and a span.

name: Gaza
description: Philistine pentapolis on the coast; Samson; Philip’s desert road.
nameMeaning: Hebrew Azzah — “strong”; linked to treasury city.

scripture.text: Now an angel of the Lord said to Philip, ‘Go south to the road—the desert road—that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.’

name: Geba
description: Benjaminite border fortress opposite Michmash.

scripture.text: Jonathan attacked the outpost at Geba, and the Philistines heard about it.

name: Gerar
description: Philistine border town of Abraham and Isaac sojourns.

scripture.text: Now there was a famine in the land—besides the previous famine in Abraham’s time—and Isaac went to Abimelek king of the Philistines in Gerar.

name: Gezer
description: Highland border fortress; Pharaoh gave it as a dowry city.

scripture.text: Here is the account of the forced labor King Solomon conscripted to build the Lord’s temple, his own palace, the terraces, the wall of Jerusalem, and Hazor, Megiddo and Gezer.

name: Gibbethon
description: Philistine border town besieged by Israel; Nadab killed.

scripture.text: Baasha son of Ahijah from the tribe of Issachar plotted against him, and he struck him down at Gibbethon, a Philistine town, while Nadab and all Israel were besieging it.

name: Gibeah of Benjamin
modernName: Jaba / Tell el-Ful area, West Bank
description: Benjaminite town; Levite’s concubine outrage; Saul’s home.

scripture.text: Saul also went to his home in Gibeah, accompanied by valiant men whose hearts God had touched.

name: Gibeon
description: Hivite highland city famous for Joshua’s sun-standing battle and Solomon’s wisdom judgment.

scripture.text: On the day the Lord gave the Amorites over to Israel, Joshua said to the Lord in the presence of Israel: ‘Sun, stand still over Gibeon, and you, moon, over the Valley of Aijalon.’

name: Gilgal (Jordan crossing)
description: Israel’s first camp in Canaan; stones of memorial; prophetic associations.

scripture.text: On the tenth day of the first month they camped at Gilgal on the eastern border of Jericho.

name: Gortyna
description: Roman capital of Crete; Titus left to set elders in every town.

scripture.text: The reason I left you in Crete was that you might put in order what was left unfinished and appoint elders in every town, as I directed you.

name: Haran
description: Caravan city where Terah settled and Jacob later served Laban.

scripture.text: So Abram went, as the Lord had told him; and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he set out from Harran.

name: Hazor
description: Major Canaanite fortification north; burned in Joshua’s wars; Solomon chariot city.

scripture.text: At that time Joshua turned back and captured Hazor and put its king to the sword. (Hazor had been the head of all these kingdoms.)

name: Hebron
description: Burial place of the patriarchs; David’s first capital in Judah.

scripture.text: After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave in the field of Machpelah near Mamre (which is at Hebron) in the land of Canaan.

name: Heshbon
description: Amorite then Moabite royal city east of the Jordan.

scripture.text: Israel captured all the cities of the Amorites and occupied them, including Heshbon and all its surrounding settlements.

name: Hierapolis
description: Phrygian spa city above Laodicea; hot springs and Apollo/Lethe cults.

scripture.text: I vouch for him that he is working hard for you and for those at Laodicea and Hierapolis.

name: Hormah
description: Place devoted to destruction (‘hormah’) after early defeat and later conquest.

scripture.text: The Lord gave the Canaanites over to them, and they destroyed them and their towns; so the place was named Hormah.

name: Ibleam
description: Jezreel corridor town; Ahaziah wounded fleeing Jehu.

scripture.text: When Ahaziah king of Judah saw what had happened, he fled up the road to Beth Haggan. Jehu chased him, shouting, ‘Kill him too!’

name: Iconium
description: Pauline mission in southern Galatia / Pisidian circuit.

scripture.text: At Iconium Paul and Barnabas went as usual into the Jewish synagogue. There they spoke so effectively that a great number of Jews and Greeks believed.

name: Jabesh Gilead
description: City that honored Saul’s bones.

scripture.text: Nahash the Ammonite went up and besieged Jabesh Gilead. And all the men of Jabesh said to him, ‘Make a treaty with us, and we will be subject to you.’

name: Jabneh (Jamnia)
description: Philistine border town; later rabbinic academy center post–AD 70.

scripture.text: He went to war against the Philistines and broke down the walls of Gath, Jabneh and Ashdod. He then rebuilt towns near Ashdod and elsewhere among the Philistines.

name: Jarmuth
description: Amorite royal city in Joshua’s southern coalition.

scripture.text: So the five kings of the Amorites—the kings of Jerusalem, Hebron, Jarmuth, Lachish and Eglon—joined forces, went up with all their troops and besieged Gibeon and attacked it.

name: Jericho
description: Ancient oasis city; first Canaanite conquest; later a border town on the road to Jerusalem.

scripture.text: When the trumpets sounded, the army shouted, and at the sound of the trumpet, when the men gave a loud shout, the wall collapsed.
Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through.

name: Jerusalem
description: The holy city, David’s capital and site of the Temple — central to Israel’s worship and to Christ’s passion and the early church.

scripture.text: Nevertheless, David captured the fortress of Zion—which is the City of David.
Carrying his own cross, he went out to the place of the Skull (which in Aramaic is called Golgotha).

name: Joppa (Jaffa)
description: Ancient coastal port; cedar logs for Temple; Peter and Tabitha.

scripture.text: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha (in Greek her name is Dorcas); she was always doing good and helping the poor.

name: Jordan River (mid-course)
description: Israel’s eastern border; crossing into Canaan; John’s baptism.

scripture.text: The priests who carried the ark of the covenant of the Lord stopped in the middle of the Jordan.
At that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

name: Kadesh Barnea
description: Desert oasis of rebellion and spy mission launching point toward Canaan.
nameMeaning: Kadesh = “holy”; Barnea likely a regional epithet.

scripture.text: They came back to Moses and Aaron and the whole Israelite community at Kadesh in the Desert of Paran. There they reported to them and to the whole assembly and showed them the fruit of the land.

name: Kedesh in Naphtali
description: Levitical city of refuge in upper Galilee; Barak’s muster point.

scripture.text: She sent for Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali and said to him, ‘The Lord, the God of Israel, commands you: “Go, take with you ten thousand men of Naphtali and Zebulun and lead them up to Mount Tabor.”’

name: Keilah
description: Judahite fortified town where David rescued inhabitants from Philistines.

scripture.text: So David inquired of the Lord, ‘Shall I go and attack these Philistines?’ The Lord answered him, ‘Go, attack the Philistines and save Keilah.’

name: Kidron Valley
description: Eastern ravine east of Jerusalem; David’s flight; Jesus crosses to Gethsemane.

scripture.text: When he had finished praying, Jesus left with his disciples and crossed the Kidron Valley. On the other side there was a garden, and he and his disciples went into it.

name: Kir Hareseth
description: Moabite stronghold; Mesha’s revolt context.

scripture.text: They destroyed the towns, and each man threw a stone on every good field until it was covered. They stopped up all the springs and cut down every good tree. Only Kir Hareseth was left with its stones in place, but men armed with slings surrounded it and attacked it.

name: Kiriath Jearim
description: Benjamite border town where the ark stayed before David brought it to Jerusalem.

scripture.text: So the men of Kiriath Jearim came and took up the ark of the Lord. They brought it to Abinadab’s house on the hill and consecrated Eleazar his son to guard the ark of the Lord.

name: Lachish
description: Fortified Judahite city; Assyrian reliefs show its siege.

scripture.text: King Hezekiah sent this message to the king of Assyria at Lachish: ‘I have done wrong. Withdraw from me, and I will pay whatever you demand of me.’

name: Laodicea
description: Wealthy textile and banking city; one of the seven churches.

scripture.text: To the angel of the church in Laodicea write: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of God’s creation.

name: Lower Beth Horon
description: Ascent road town pair with Upper Beth Horon; Joshua’s battle.

scripture.text: went down westward to the territory of the Japhletites as far as the region of Lower Beth Horon and on to Gezer, which ended at the Mediterranean Sea.

name: Lydda (Lod)
description: Coastal plain town; Aeneas healed; NT Lydda.

scripture.text: As Peter traveled about the country, he went to visit the Lord’s people who lived in Lydda.

name: Lystra
description: Galatian mission city; Paul stoned and left for dead.

scripture.text: In Lystra there sat a man who was lame. He had been that way from birth and had never walked.

name: Magdala
description: Fishing village; Mary Magdalene’s traditional home.

scripture.text: Mary called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had come out;

name: Mahanaim
description: Jacob’s angel camp; Ishbosheth’s capital; Solomon district.

scripture.text: When Jacob saw them, he said, ‘This is the camp of God!’ So he named that place Mahanaim.

name: Malta (Melita)
description: Shipwreck island; Publius’s father healed.
nameMeaning: Honey (meli) folk etymology; island name Latin Melita.

scripture.text: Once safely on shore, we found out that the island was called Malta.

name: Megiddo
description: Strategic Jezreel pass fortress; Josiah’s death; symbolic of final battle (Har-Magedon).

scripture.text: Josiah, however, would not turn away from him, but disguised himself to engage him in battle. He would not listen to what Necho had said at God’s command but went to fight him on the plain of Megiddo.

name: Memphis (Egypt)
description: Old Kingdom capital near the Nile delta — context for Israel in Egypt.

scripture.text: Take some carts from Egypt for your children and your wives, and get your father and come.

name: Michmash
description: Benjaminite gorge town; Jonathan’s daring raid.

scripture.text: One cliff stood to the north toward Michmash, the other to the south toward Geba.

name: Midian
description: Ishmaelite-related peoples; Moses’ refuge; Gideon’s conflict.
nameMeaning: From Midian son of Abraham and Keturah.

scripture.text: When Pharaoh heard of this, he tried to kill Moses, but Moses fled from Pharaoh and went to live in Midian, where he sat down by a well.

name: Miletus
description: Paul’s farewell to Ephesian elders.

scripture.text: From Miletus, Paul sent to Ephesus for the elders of the church.

name: Mizpah (Benjamin)
modernName: Tell en-Nasbeh / Nebi Samwil area (debated), West Bank
description: Benjaminite watch-height; often distinguished from Mizpah in Gilead.

scripture.text: Then all Israel from Dan to Beersheba and from the land of Gilead came together as one and assembled before the Lord in Mizpah.

name: Mizpah in Gilead
description: Jephthah’s assembly; watchtower name reused in many sites.

scripture.text: So Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the people made him head and commander over them. And he repeated all his words before the Lord in Mizpah.

name: Moab
description: Lot’s descendants east of the Dead Sea; Ruth’s story; Balak and Balaam.
nameMeaning: From Lot’s son Moab — “from father” (ironic etymology in Gen 19:37).

scripture.text: In the days when the judges ruled, there was a famine in the land. So a man from Bethlehem in Judah, together with his wife and two sons, went to live for a while in the country of Moab.

name: Mount Carmel
description: Coastal ridge; Elijah confronts the prophets of Baal.

scripture.text: Now summon the people from all over Israel to meet me on Mount Carmel.

name: Mount Gilboa
description: Ridge where Saul and Jonathan died fighting Philistines.

scripture.text: Now the Philistines fought against Israel; the Israelites fled before them, and many fell dead on Mount Gilboa.

name: Mount Hermon
description: Anti-Lebanon snow peak; northern boundary imagery; Transfiguration tradition (region).
nameMeaning: Forbidden / devoted (herem) or Semitic mountain deity echoes (debated).

scripture.text: It is as if the dew of Hermon were falling on Mount Zion. For there the Lord bestows his blessing, even life forevermore.

name: Mount Nebo
description: Moses viewed the promised land; traditional burial site nearby.

scripture.text: Then Moses climbed Mount Nebo from the plains of Moab to the top of Pisgah, across from Jericho. There the Lord showed him the whole land.

name: Mount Sinai (Horeb)
description: Mountain of God where Moses received the covenant.

scripture.text: The Lord descended to the top of Mount Sinai and called Moses to the top of the mountain.

name: Mount Tabor
description: Isolated Galilean hill; Deborah and Barak battle tradition.

scripture.text: She sent for Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali and said to him, ‘The Lord, the God of Israel, commands you: “Go, take with you ten thousand men of Naphtali and Zebulun and lead them up to Mount Tabor.”’

name: Mountains of Ararat
description: Traditional resting place of Noah’s ark after the flood.

scripture.text: On the seventeenth day of the seventh month the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.

name: Myra
description: Lycian capital port; centurion’s Alexandrian grain ship.

scripture.text: When we had sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we landed at Myra in Lycia.

name: Nain
description: Galilean village; widow’s son raised.

scripture.text: Soon afterward, Jesus went to a town called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd went along with him.

name: Nazareth
description: Jesus’ boyhood home in lower Galilee.

scripture.text: When Joseph and Mary had done everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee to their own town of Nazareth.
He went to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue, as was his custom.

name: Neapolis (Macedonia)
description: Harbor where Paul first set foot in Europe before Philippi.

scripture.text: From Troas we put out to sea and sailed straight for Samothrace, and the next day we went to Neapolis.

name: Nicopolis
description: Augustan victory city; Paul wintered and planned ministry (tradition).

scripture.text: As soon as I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, because I have decided to winter there.

name: Nineveh
description: Assyrian imperial capital; Jonah’s preaching and Nahum’s oracle.
nameMeaning: Akkadian Nina/Ninua (linked to a goddess); Hebrew Nineveh — exact etymology uncertain in Scripture.

scripture.text: Jonah obeyed the word of the Lord and went to Nineveh. Now Nineveh was a very large city; it took three days to go through it.
A prophecy concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.

name: Nob
description: Priestly town massacred by Saul; David took Goliath’s sword.

scripture.text: Saul also attacked the priests, and the Lord put them to death for their complicity with David.

name: Ophel (Jerusalem ridge)
description: Ridge south of the Temple; fortifications and Levitical dwellings.

scripture.text: Jotham rebuilt the Upper Gate of the temple of the Lord and did extensive work on the wall at the hill of Ophel.

name: Patara
description: Lycian harbor; Paul changes ships for Tyre.

scripture.text: After we had torn ourselves away from them, we put out to sea and sailed straight to Cos. The next day we went to Rhodes and from there to Patara.

name: Patmos
description: Volcanic island of John’s exile and Revelation.

scripture.text: I, John, your brother and companion in the suffering and kingdom and patient endurance that are ours in Jesus, was on the island of Patmos because of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus.

name: Peniel (Penuel)
description: Jacob wrestled at the Jabbok ford; later Gideon story.

scripture.text: So Jacob called the place Peniel, saying, ‘It is because I saw God face to face, and yet my life was spared.’

name: Perga
description: Pamphylian capital; John Mark left Paul and Barnabas here.

scripture.text: From Paphos, Paul and his companions sailed to Perga in Pamphylia, where John left them to return to Jerusalem.

name: Pergamum
description: Royal Asian capital; altar imagery in Revelation.

scripture.text: To the angel of the church in Pergamum write: These are the words of him who has the sharp, double-edged sword.

name: Phaselis
description: Lycian harbor city on Pamphylian coast; possible coasting point.

scripture.text: When we had sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we landed at Myra in Lycia.

name: Philadelphia (Asia)
description: Missionary frontier city; ‘open door’ promise.

scripture.text: To the angel of the church in Philadelphia write: These are the words of him who is holy and true, who holds the key of David.

name: Philippi
description: Roman colony in Macedonia; Lydia and the jailer.

scripture.text: From there we traveled to Philippi, a Roman colony and the leading city of that district of Macedonia.

name: Philistia
description: Sea Peoples pentapolis league along the southern Levant coast.

scripture.text: These are the nations the Lord left to test all those Israelites who had not experienced any of the wars in Canaan — the five rulers of the Philistines, all the Canaanites, the Sidonians, and the Hivites living in the Lebanon mountains from Mount Baal Hermon to Lebo Hamath.

name: Phoenix (Crete)
description: Winter harbor goal that Paul’s ship never reached before the gale.

scripture.text: Since the harbor was unsuitable to winter in, the majority decided that we should sail on, hoping to reach Phoenix and winter there. This was a harbor in Crete, facing both southwest and northwest.

name: Pool of Siloam
description: Hezekiah’s tunnel outlet; Jesus sends blind man to wash.

scripture.text: ‘Go,’ he told him, ‘wash in the Pool of Siloam’ (this word means ‘Sent’). So the man went and washed, and came home seeing.

name: Put (Libya)
description: North African people listed with Cush and Lud in prophetic oracles.

scripture.text: Men of Persia, Lud and Put served as soldiers in your army.

name: Puteoli
description: Bay of Naples harbor where Roman Christians met Paul’s ship.

scripture.text: From there we set sail and arrived at Rhegium. The next day the south wind came up, and on the following day we reached Puteoli.

name: Rabbah (Amman)
description: Capital of Ammon; Uriah’s death; later Philadelphia (Hellenistic).

scripture.text: In the spring, at the time when kings go off to war, David sent Joab out with the king’s men and the whole Israelite army. They destroyed the Ammonites and besieged Rabbah. But David remained in Jerusalem.

name: Ramah (Benjamin)
description: Benjaminite town north of Jerusalem; Samuel’s circuit; Jeremiah’s release.

scripture.text: But he always went back to Ramah, where his home was, and there he also held court for Israel. And he built an altar there to the Lord.

name: Ramathaim (Zophim)
modernName: Ar-Ram / Nabi Samwil area, West Bank
description: Hill country of Ephraim; Samuel’s family hometown tradition.

scripture.text: There was a certain man from Ramathaim, a Zuphite from the hill country of Ephraim, whose name was Elkanah son of Jeroham, the son of Elihu, the son of Tohu, the son of Zuph, an Ephraimite.

name: Ramoth in Gilead
description: Levitical city of refuge east of Jordan; Ahab’s death in battle.

scripture.text: The Lord said, ‘Who will entice Ahab into attacking Ramoth Gilead and going to his death there?’

name: Red Sea (crossing region)
description: Traditional approximate region for Israel’s escape through the sea.

scripture.text: Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and all that night the Lord drove the sea back with a strong east wind.

name: Rhegium (Regium)
description: Strait of Messina toehold; Paul waits for favorable wind to Puteoli.

scripture.text: From there we set sail and arrived at Rhegium. The next day the south wind came up, and on the following day we reached Puteoli.

name: Rhodes
description: Dodecanese island; Paul’s return voyage touches here.

scripture.text: After we had torn ourselves away from them, we put out to sea and sailed straight to Cos. The next day we went to Rhodes and from there to Patara.

name: Rome
description: Imperial capital; destination of Paul’s chains and Peter’s ministry tradition.

scripture.text: There we found some brothers and sisters who invited us to spend a week with them. And so we came to Rome.

name: Salamis (Cyprus)
description: Cyprus port city; Paul and Barnabas preached in synagogues.
nameMeaning: Named for Salamis the Greek hero (tradition).

scripture.text: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their helper.

name: Samaria (city)
description: Capital of the northern kingdom; later a Samaritan center.

scripture.text: He bought the hill of Samaria from Shemer for two talents of silver and built a city on the hill, calling it Samaria.
So he came to a town in Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of ground Jacob had given to his son Joseph.

name: Samos
description: Hera cult island off Ephesus; Paul passes opposite.

scripture.text: Sailing from there, we arrived the next day opposite Chios. The day after that we crossed over to Samos, and on the following day arrived at Miletus.

name: Samothrace
description: Aegean island cult center of Cabeiri; Paul’s overnight stop.

scripture.text: From Troas we put out to sea and sailed straight for Samothrace, and the next day we went to Neapolis.

name: Sardis
description: Lydian capital; dead reputation in Revelation.

scripture.text: These are the words of him who holds the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. I know your deeds; you have a reputation of being alive, but you are dead.

name: Sea of Galilee
description: Freshwater lake; center of Jesus’ Galilean ministry.

scripture.text: As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon called Peter and his brother Andrew.

name: Shaaraim
description: Shephelah town; Philistines fled toward it in David and Goliath narrative.

scripture.text: Then the men of Israel and Judah surged forward with a shout and pursued the Philistines as far as Gath and Ekron, striking them down along the way to Shaaraim as far as Gath and Ekron.

name: Shechem
description: Central highland city between Mount Ebal and Gerizim; covenant and well narratives; later Samaritan center.
events: Jesus and the woman at Sychar (nearby)

scripture.text: Abram traveled through the land as far as the site of the great tree of Moreh at Shechem. At that time the Canaanites were in the land.

name: Shiloh
description: Ephraimite sanctuary where the tabernacle and ark dwelt in the judges period.
nameMeaning: Often understood as “rest” or “he who is sent.”

scripture.text: Year after year this man went up from his town to worship and sacrifice to the Lord Almighty at Shiloh, where Hophni and Phinehas, the two sons of Eli, were priests of the Lord.

name: Shunem
description: Jezreel ridge village; Elisha’s upper room and Shunammite woman.

scripture.text: One day Elisha went to Shunem. And a well-to-do woman was there, who urged him to stay for a meal. So whenever he came by, he stopped there to eat.

name: Sidon
description: Northern Phoenician city; associated with Canaanite culture and Jesus’ ministry borders.
nameMeaning: Tradition links to “fishing” or a founder Sidon.
events: Jesus in Tyre and Sidon region

scripture.text: Leaving that place, Jesus withdrew to the region of Tyre and Sidon.

name: Smyrna
description: Wealthy port; Polycarp’s martyrdom tradition; suffering church.

scripture.text: To the angel of the church in Smyrna write: These are the words of him who is the First and the Last, who died and came to life again.

name: Socoh
description: Shephelah town overlooking Elah valley; Philistine muster against Saul.

scripture.text: Now the Philistines gathered their forces for war and assembled at Sokoh in Judah.

name: Sodom (traditional region)
description: Cities of the plain judged for wickedness alongside Gomorrah.
nameMeaning: Uncertain; possibly linked to Hebrew sedeh / balloting or foreign root.

scripture.text: Then the Lord rained down burning sulfur on Sodom and Gomorrah—from the Lord out of the heavens.

name: Succoth
description: Transjordan settlement; Jacob builds booths; Gideon teaches elders.

scripture.text: Jacob, however, went to Sukkoth, where he built a place for himself and made shelters for his livestock. That is why the place is called Sukkoth.

name: Susa (Shushan)
description: Persian royal city; Esther and Daniel’s visions context.
nameMeaning: City name from Elamite/Persian roots (lily / horse?).

scripture.text: At that time King Xerxes reigned from his royal throne in the citadel of Susa,

name: Sychar (near Shechem)
description: Jacob’s well; Samaritan woman conversation.

scripture.text: So he came to a town in Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of ground Jacob had given to his son Joseph.

name: Syracuse
description: Sicilian Greek powerhouse; Paul’s Alexandrian ship put in.

scripture.text: We put in at Syracuse and stayed there three days.

name: Taanach
description: Canaanite town controlling Megiddo pass; Deborah’s victory.

scripture.text: Kings came, they fought, the kings of Canaan fought at Taanach by the waters of Megiddo, but they carried off no silver, no plunder.

name: Tarshish (trad. Spain)
description: Distant western port in prophetic imagination; Jonah fled toward Tarshish.
nameMeaning: Uncertain; often linked to Tartessos in Spain.

scripture.text: But Jonah ran away from the Lord and headed for Tarshish. He went down to Joppa, where he found a ship bound for that port.

name: Tekoa
description: Judahite hill town; home of the ‘wise woman’ and prophet Amos (tradition).

scripture.text: So Joab sent someone to Tekoa and had a wise woman brought from there. He said to her, ‘Pretend you are in mourning. Dress in mourning clothes, and don’t use any cosmetic lotions.’

name: Thessalonica
description: Key Macedonian port; two Pauline letters.

scripture.text: When Paul and his companions had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica.

name: Three Taverns
description: Appian Way stop closer to Rome; second welcoming party.
nameMeaning: Latin Tres Tabernae — three shops / inns.
events: Paul encouraged en route to Rome

scripture.text: At the sight of these people Paul thanked God and was encouraged.

name: Thyatira
description: Lydia’s hometown trade guilds; Jezebel teaching warned.

scripture.text: To the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These are the words of the Son of God, whose eyes are like blazing fire and whose feet are like burnished bronze.

name: Tiberias
description: Herodian capital on Galilee’s west shore; John’s Gospel uses the sea’s Roman name.

scripture.text: Then some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

name: Timnah (Sorek valley)
description: Philistine border town; Samson and Delilah narrative.

scripture.text: Some time later, he fell in love with a woman in the Valley of Sorek whose name was Delilah.

name: Tirzah
description: Early capital of northern kingdom before Samaria.

scripture.text: Jeroboam’s wife arose and departed and came to Tirzah. As she came to the threshold of the house, the child died.

name: Troas (Alexandria Troas)
description: Paul’s vision of the Macedonian; Eutychus raised.

scripture.text: So they passed by Mysia and went down to Troas.

name: Tyre
description: Phoenician maritime power; Tyrian purple; prophetic oracles; Jesus withdrew here.

scripture.text: Son of man, because Tyre has said of Jerusalem, ‘Aha! The gate to the nations is broken, and its doors have swung open to me; now that she lies in ruins I will prosper,’

name: Upper Beth Horon
description: Strategic ascent to central highlands; Joshua’s long day battle linked to Aijalon valley.
nameMeaning: House of Horon (deity name in Canaanite context).

scripture.text: The Lord threw them into confusion before Israel, so great a defeat at Gibeon that they fled from before Israel, toward the road to Beth Horon.

name: Ur of the Chaldeans
description: Astral-worshiping city-state; Abraham’s departure point.

scripture.text: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, and they set out from Ur of the Chaldeans for Canaan.

name: Wilderness of Sinai
description: Desert region where Israel wandered and received the law.

scripture.text: On the first day of the third month after the Israelites left Egypt—on that very day—they came to the Desert of Sinai.

name: Wilderness of Ziph
modernName: Hills southeast of Hebron, West Bank
description: Judean wilderness where David hid from Saul.

scripture.text: David stayed in the wilderness strongholds and in the hills of the Desert of Ziph. Day after day Saul searched for him, but God did not give David into his hands.

name: Ziklag
description: Philistine border town given to David while he served Achish.
nameMeaning: Possibly “pressing” or place-name of uncertain root.

scripture.text: So on that day Achish gave him Ziklag, and it has belonged to the kings of Judah ever since.

name: Zoar
modernName: Ghor as-Safi / southeast Dead Sea, Jordan
description: Small ‘town’ spared with Sodom and Gomorrah; Lot’s refuge.

scripture.text: But flee there quickly, because I cannot do anything until you reach it. (That is why the town was called Zoar.)

name: Zorah
description: Danite town; Manoah’s home and Samson’s upbringing.

scripture.text: A certain man of Zorah, named Manoah, from the clan of the Danites, had a wife who was childless, unable to give birth.

name: Antioch of Pisidia
description: Pauline city in southern Galatia / Pisidia.

name: Chorazin
description: Galilean village named with Bethsaida and Capernaum.

name: Crete
description: Mediterranean island; Paul’s voyage and Titus’s charge.

name: Valley of Elah
description: Valley where David met Goliath.

name: Elim
description: Israel camped at Elim after Marah (Exodus 15:27).

name: Gethsemane
description: Garden at the foot of the Mount of Olives.

name: Gilead
description: Region east of the Jordan associated with Jacob, Jephthah, and exile traffic.

name: Golgotha
description: Place of the skull; crucifixion of Jesus.

name: Goshen
description: Pastoral land where Israel settled in Egypt.

name: Heshbon
description: Amorite royal city later claimed by Israel and Moab.

name: Jezreel
description: Valley and fortress city linked to Deborah, Saul, and Jehu.

name: Kidron Valley
description: Eastern ravine between Jerusalem and the Mount of Olives.

name: Marah
description: Bitter waters turned sweet after the sea crossing.

name: Mitylene
description: Paul touched at Mitylene sailing south (Acts 20:14).

name: Mount of Olives
description: Ridge east of Jerusalem; David’s flight and Jesus’ teaching.

name: Negev
description: Southern wilderness of Judah and Abraham’s sojourns.

name: Paphos
description: Roman capital of Cyprus; Sergius Paulus and Elymas (Acts 13).

name: Petra
description: Nabataean capital; often linked with Arabia in Pauline tradition.

name: Rameses
description: Store-city and Israel’s departure point (Exodus 12:37).

name: Rephidim
description: Wilderness camp before Sinai; water from the rock.

name: Seleucia Pieria
description: Harbor of Syrian Antioch; Paul sailed from here (Acts 13:4).

name: Tahpanhes
description: Frontier town where Jeremiah’s community fled (Jeremiah 43–44).

name: Wilderness of Sin
description: Israel’s camp where manna was first given (Exodus 16).
